mission.title: helm: Add 'helm install --app-version' command/versioning a chart against an app version
mission.description: Add 'helm install --app-version' command/versioning a chart against an app version. This issue affects 184+ users.

steps.title: Identify helm deploy symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your helm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=helm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add 'helm install --app-version' command/versioning a chart against an app version

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
As far as I'm aware, `helm install` currently only supports specifying the `--version` flag to specify a chart version to install.

I'm unsure how the 'appVersion' field in a Chart.yaml is supposed to

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add 'helm install --app-version' command/versioning a chart
steps.description:
* Automated cherry pick of #522 (jetstack/cert-manager#615)
```yaml
$ helm package myapp --app-version 1.0.0
$ helm install myapp-1.0.0.tgz
```

steps.title: Confirm Add 'helm install --app-version' command/versionin is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: * Automated cherry pick of #522 (jetstack/cert-manager#615). This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
$ helm package myapp --app-version 1.0.0
$ helm install myapp-1.0.0.tgz

$ helm package myapp --app-version 1.0.0
$ helm install myapp-1.0.0.tgz

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
